data.description: OpenAI and the Gates Foundation unveil Horizon 1000, a $50 million program backing African health leaders with funding and technology.

data.title: Ahrefs launches AI prompt tracking in Brand Radar to monitor brand visibility in AI responses; ChatGPT reaches hundreds of millions weekly.
data.description: A popular marketing platform, Ahrefs, unveiled a new feature called custom AI prompt tracking in Brand Radar, designed to help teams monitor their brand's visibility in AI-generated responses on platforms such as ChatGPT, Gemini, and Perplexity. With AI assistants emerging as a primary discovery channel, companies are keen on determining their brand's presence in AI-generated answers. According to OpenAI, ChatGPT now engages with hundreds of millions of users every week.

data.title: Transparency in crypto trading key as Zoomex prioritizes asset custody and withdrawals.
data.description: In Victoria, Seychelles on January 21, 2026, the significance of transparency in crypto trading is on the rise with platforms being evaluated based on their handling of asset custody, settlement, and withdrawals. These aspects have become more critical for traders, surpassing product complexity in importance. This shift reflects the growing emphasis on clear and secure processes within the industry.

data.title: Japanese bond market instability impacts global liquidity, causing pressure on risk assets. Concerns rise over tighter financial conditions.
data.description: Market turbulence in Japan's bond market has had a ripple effect worldwide, impacting risk assets and prompting worries about more restrictive financial conditions.

data.title: 2026 Japan Prize Laureates announced: Prof. Cynthia Dwork (USA) for Electronics, Information, and Communication, Prof. Shizuo Akira (Japan) and Prof. Zhijian "James" Chen (USA) for Life Sciences.
data.description: In Tokyo, the Japan Prize Foundation revealed the recipients of the 2026 Japan Prize at 1:00 p.m. (JST) on January 21, 2026. Prof. Cynthia Dwork from the USA was honored with the Japan Prize in Electronics, Information, and Communication. Additionally, Prof. Shizuo Akira from Japan and Prof. Zhijian "James" Chen from the USA were awarded the Japan Prize in Life Sciences. Prof. Dwork is acknowledged for her significant contributions to pioneering research, making her stand out among this year's Japan Prize laureates.

data.title: SoftBank Corp. introduces Infrinia AI Cloud OS for AI data centers, enabling KaaS and Inf-aaS in a multi-tenant environment.
data.description: SoftBank Corp revealed that its Infrinia Team has created "Infrinia AI Cloud OS," a software stack tailored for AI data centers. This innovation allows AI data center operators to establish Kubernetes as a Service (KaaS) and Inference as a Service (Inf-aaS) in a multi-tenant setting. The company, listed as TOKYO:9434 and led by President & CEO Junichi Miyakawa, specializes in developing next-generation AI infrastructure architecture and systems. This advancement underscores SoftBank's commitment to enhancing AI technology in data centers, marking a significant milestone in the field.
